summary.headline: On-device AI inference surges with Google LiteRT-LM and AI Edge Gallery launches; agentic frameworks proliferate across coding, learning, and security; open-source LLM tooling (Ollama, llama.cpp, Goose) continues rapid adoption
summary.body:
Today's trending landscape is dominated by **on-device and edge AI inference**, with Google releasing both `LiteRT-LM` (a C++ runtime for on-device LLM inference) and an `AI Edge Gallery` (Kotlin-based showcase for local ML/GenAI use cases). These releases signal a major push from Google to bring frontier model capabilities to mobile and embedded hardware without cloud dependency. Alongside these, NVIDIA's `PersonaPlex` adds persona-driven synthetic data generation, and NousResearch's `hermes-agent` framework is capturing massive community attention.

The **agentic AI** theme is impossible to ignore: repos like `block/goose` (extensible AI agent in Rust), `HKUDS/DeepTutor` (agent-native personalized learning), `KeygraphHQ/shannon` (autonomous AI pentester), and `abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus` (client-side Graph RAG for codebases) all reflect a fast-maturing ecosystem where LLM-backed agents are being deployed in specialized, high-stakes domains. The proliferation of Claude Code skill sets (Jeffallan and kepano) further underscores that developer-facing agent augmentation is entering mainstream tooling.

With no HuggingFace Daily Papers or trending models surfacing today, the signal is concentrated entirely in GitHub momentum — suggesting today is more a **practitioner and tooling day** than a foundational research day. The stars-today anomalies (all repos showing `Stars: 16` despite wildly different `Stars_today` values) indicate a data collection artifact; stars_today figures are taken as authoritative for ranking purposes.

researcher_notes:
**Data Quality Note:** All GitHub repos in today's dataset show `Stars: 16` and `Forks: 16` — this is clearly a scraping/normalization artifact. The `stars_today` field is the only reliable signal for today's trending rank. Repos are ranked and analyzed on that basis. Real cumulative star counts for well-known repos like `ollama/ollama`, `ggml-org/llama.cpp`, and `donnemartin/system-design-primer` are in the tens of thousands; treat the `16` value as a placeholder error.

**Non-obvious connection — Edge AI convergence:** Google releasing both `LiteRT-LM` (C++ inference runtime) and `AI Edge Gallery` (Kotlin demo app) on the same trending day is not a coincidence. This is a coordinated ecosystem push: LiteRT-LM provides the inference substrate, and the Gallery provides the developer-facing showcase. Combined with `Blaizzy/mlx-vlm` for Apple Silicon and `ollama/ollama` supporting Kimi-K2.5/GLM-5/MiniMax, we are witnessing **simultaneous cross-platform local inference maturation** — Apple, Google, and the open-source community are all converging on making sub-70B models run efficiently on consumer hardware in Q1-Q2 2026.

**Sleeper hit — `tobi/qmd`:** With 526 stars today, this mini CLI search engine for local docs is quietly solving a real pain point: semantic search over private knowledge bases without cloud APIs. It explicitly tracks 'current SOTA approaches while being all local.' As RAG fatigue sets in with heavyweight frameworks, lightweight local-first alternatives like qmd may see outsized adoption among developers who want pragmatic tooling without vendor lock-in.

**Agent specialization is accelerating:** The gap between general-purpose agents (Goose, hermes-agent) and domain-specific agents (Shannon for pentesting, DeepTutor for education, GitNexus for code intelligence) is narrowing in terms of capability but widening in terms of deployment context. This suggests the next wave of value creation will come from **fine-grained domain adaptation** of agent scaffolding rather than new base model capabilities. The Obsidian and Claude Code skill ecosystems (kepano, Jeffallan) are early indicators of a 'skill marketplace' model emerging around frontier coding assistants.

**NVIDIA PersonaPlex** is worth watching closely: synthetic persona generation for training data is a relatively underexplored vector for improving model alignment and diversity. If PersonaPlex enables high-fidelity, controllable persona injection into synthetic datasets, it could become a critical component of the post-RLHF training pipeline for enterprise LLMs — particularly for customer-facing applications where persona consistency matters.

trending_repos.name: NousResearch/hermes-agent
trending_repos.description: A growing, extensible AI agent framework from NousResearch built around the Hermes model family. Garnered extraordinary community attention on launch day, suggesting strong developer enthusiasm for Hermes-native agentic tooling.

trending_repos.name: siddharthvaddem/openscreen
trending_repos.description: Open-source, no-watermark demo recording tool positioned as a free alternative to Screen Studio. Viral launch with 1823 stars today; not directly AI but widely used in AI demo workflows.

trending_repos.name: block/goose
trending_repos.description: An open-source, extensible AI agent written in Rust that goes beyond code suggestions to install, execute, edit, and test — compatible with any LLM. Strong momentum as a production-grade autonomous coding agent.

trending_repos.name: google-ai-edge/gallery
trending_repos.description: Google AI Edge's official gallery showcasing on-device ML and GenAI use cases, allowing users to run and test models locally on Android. Part of Google's coordinated edge AI ecosystem push alongside LiteRT-LM.

trending_repos.name: abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus
trending_repos.description: A client-side, zero-server code intelligence engine that runs entirely in the browser, creating interactive knowledge graphs from GitHub repos with a built-in Graph RAG agent. Novel browser-native RAG approach for code exploration.

trending_repos.name: onyx-dot-app/onyx
trending_repos.description: Open-source AI chat platform with advanced features supporting every major LLM provider. Broad LLM compatibility and enterprise-grade features driving sustained community interest.

trending_repos.name: KeygraphHQ/shannon
trending_repos.description: Shannon Lite is an autonomous white-box AI pentester for web applications and APIs that analyzes source code, identifies attack vectors, and executes real exploits. Represents the cutting edge of agentic security tooling.

trending_repos.name: google-ai-edge/LiteRT-LM
trending_repos.description: Google's C++ runtime for on-device LLM inference, part of the LiteRT (formerly TFLite) ecosystem. Enables efficient local LLM execution on mobile and embedded devices.

trending_repos.name: tobi/qmd
trending_repos.description: A minimal CLI search engine for local docs, knowledge bases, and meeting notes using SOTA local embedding/retrieval approaches with no cloud dependency. A pragmatic local-first alternative to heavy RAG frameworks.

trending_repos.name: NVIDIA/personaplex
trending_repos.description: NVIDIA's PersonaPlex framework for persona-driven synthetic data generation. Enables controllable persona injection into training pipelines, relevant for alignment and enterprise LLM fine-tuning.

trending_repos.name: Blaizzy/mlx-vlm
trending_repos.description: MLX-VLM provides inference and fine-tuning for Vision Language Models on Apple Silicon Macs using the MLX framework. Consistently trending as on-device VLM interest grows.

trending_repos.name: ggml-org/llama.cpp
trending_repos.description: The gold-standard C/C++ LLM inference library enabling fast local inference across hardware backends. Ongoing community momentum as new models (Kimi-K2.5, GLM-5) are integrated.

trending_repos.name: HKUDS/DeepTutor
trending_repos.description: Agent-native personalized learning assistant from Hong Kong University of Science. Applies agentic LLM workflows to adaptive education, representing a growing vertical AI application domain.

trending_repos.name: ollama/ollama
trending_repos.description: The leading open-source tool for running LLMs locally, now supporting Kimi-K2.5, GLM-5, MiniMax, DeepSeek, GPT-OSS, Qwen, Gemma, and more. Continuous model additions drive sustained trending.

trending_repos.name: donnemartin/system-design-primer
trending_repos.description: Classic resource for learning large-scale system design with Anki flashcards. Perennially trending; renewed interest likely driven by AI system design interview prep.

themes.name: On-Device and Edge AI Inference
themes.description: Google's simultaneous release of LiteRT-LM and AI Edge Gallery, combined with MLX-VLM and Ollama's expanding model support, marks a coordinated multi-platform push to make frontier LLMs run locally without cloud dependency.

themes.name: Agentic Frameworks and Vertical Agents
themes.description: A proliferation of both general-purpose agents (hermes-agent, goose) and domain-specialized agents (Shannon for security, DeepTutor for education, GitNexus for code) signals rapid maturation of the agentic application layer.

themes.name: Open-Source LLM Inference Tooling
themes.description: llama.cpp, Ollama, and MLX-VLM continue to trend as community standards for local LLM inference, with new model integrations (Kimi-K2.5, GLM-5, MiniMax) driving fresh adoption cycles.

themes.name: Developer AI Augmentation and Skill Ecosystems
themes.description: Skill packs for Claude Code (Jeffallan, kepano/Obsidian) and the NotebookLM Python API reflect a growing ecosystem of developer-facing LLM augmentation tools that extend existing workflows rather than replace them.

themes.name: Synthetic Data and Persona Generation
themes.description: NVIDIA's PersonaPlex highlights growing interest in controlled synthetic data generation with persona fidelity, relevant for enterprise fine-tuning and alignment research.

themes.name: Local-First RAG and Knowledge Graph Intelligence
themes.description: Tools like GitNexus (browser-native Graph RAG) and qmd (CLI local search) represent a pragmatic counter-movement to heavyweight cloud RAG frameworks, prioritizing privacy and zero-server architectures.
